109.reason: [The condition for the multi-line comment loop is incorrect. It should be `!(input.charAt(pos) == '*' && input.charAt(pos + 1) == '/')` to properly detect the end of the comment. The current condition will exit the loop prematurely if it encounters a '*' not followed by '/', and will also cause an index out-of-bounds error when `pos + 1` is out of range.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition for ending multi-line comments is incorrect; it should check for "*/" as a pair, but the loop condition only checks for '*' followed by '/' without properly advancing when '*' is found, potentially causing an infinite loop or incorrect termination.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition for the multi-line comment loop is incorrect. It should be `!(input.charAt(pos) == '*' && input.charAt(pos + 1) == '/')` to properly detect the end of the comment. The current condition will exit the loop prematurely if it encounters a '*' not followed by '/', and will also cause an index out-of-bounds error when `pos + 1` is out of range.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition is incorrect: it stops only when both conditions are false, but it should stop when "*/" is found. Also, after the loop, it does not move past the closing "*/". This can cause infinite loops or incorrect token recognition.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing bounds check for pos+1 causes IndexOutOfBoundsException at EOF; also, checking pos != '*' prematurely terminates block comment skipping when a lone '*' appears.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing bounds check for pos+1 before accessing charAt, which causes IndexOutOfBoundsException at EOF
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition `input.charAt(pos + 1) != '/'` may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException when `pos` is at the last character; also, this loop fails to properly skip multi-line comments because it stops too early—should continue until "*/" is found.]

175.reason: After handling the logical operator, the code moves an extra position even when the operator is a single character (error case), which skips the next character incorrectly.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  After handling the logical operator, the code moves an extra position even when the operator is a single character (error case), which skips the next character incorrectly.

215.reason: Error: condition order causes potential StringIndexOutOfBoundsException because `input.charAt(pos + 1)` is evaluated before checking `pos + 1 >= input.length()`. Should be `(pos + 1 >= input.length() || input.charAt(pos + 1) != '=')`.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition for '!' is incorrectly placed here; '!' should be handled as a comparison operator (for "!=") or as NOT, but this logic may misclassify it and also has an out-of-bounds risk when pos+1 is at the end.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: condition order causes potential StringIndexOutOfBoundsException because `input.charAt(pos + 1)` is evaluated before checking `pos + 1 >= input.length()`. Should be `(pos + 1 >= input.length() || input.charAt(pos + 1) != '=')`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  input.charAt(pos + 1) is evaluated before the bounds check, causing IndexOutOfBoundsException when '!' is at the end of the file.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Bounds check pos+1 >= input.length() is evaluated after charAt(pos+1), causing IndexOutOfBoundsException when '!' is the last character
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition for '!' is incorrect: it treats '!' as an arithmetic operator when it should be handled as a comparison or logical NOT operator. Also, accessing input.charAt(pos + 1) may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException when pos + 1 == input.length().]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The logic for handling '!' is misplaced here—it should not be considered an arithmetic operator. Moreover, the condition is inverted: '!' followed by '=' forms "!=" (a comparison operator), so a standalone '!' is NOT, but this function incorrectly tries to classify '!' as arithmetic, leading to misclassification and potential failure to detect error type 'a' for single '&' or '|'.]

174.reason: Unconditionally consumes the next character after detecting a single '&' or '|', leading to token loss and incorrect parsing

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Unconditionally consumes the next character after detecting a single '&' or '|', leading to token loss and incorrect parsing

256.reason: [Because '!' is incorrectly classified as an arithmetic operator, it will be passed to getArithmeticOperator, which maps it to TokenType.NOT via default in getArithmeticOperatorType—but this bypasses proper handling of "!=" and standalone "!". Moreover, the logic order causes "!=" to never be recognized because '!' is consumed as an arithmetic operator before comparison logic runs.]
